netforce.hint: Add or remove people, the trolley will move!

guides: What is friction? → When you push an object, the ground or surface tries to stop it. This resisting force is friction.
When will it move? → The object moves only when your applied force is greater than the friction force.
The box is light, so low friction. It will move with just ~80N.
The fridge is heavier than the box; it needs about 270N to move.
The car is the heaviest; it needs about 590N or more to move!
What if friction is OFF? → Like an icy floor! Even the smallest force will keep the object moving.
With friction ON, the object won't move unless you apply enough force, just like in real life.

objectTips.ice: Ice is very light and slippery, moves with just 5N!
objectTips.box: Apply about 80N to move the box.
objectTips.fridge: The fridge is heavy! Apply about 270N or more to move it.
objectTips.car: The car is the heaviest! Apply about 590N or more to move it!

motion.hint: Slide to apply force and watch when the object starts moving!

friction.hint: Increase force, when does it start moving?
guides: The rougher the surface, the higher the friction!
The object moves only after overcoming maximum static friction.

tug.hint: Add people to teams, which side will pull the rope?
guides: The cart moves towards the team with more net force!
If force is equal on both sides, the cart stays still.
